rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Capacity building of DPOs and empowerment women with disabilities
rows.description: Projects by local DPOs to build their capacity and improve employment facilities leading to more independent living of persons with disabilities, particularly women with disabilities.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Country Program 2018
rows.description:
Abilis Foundation covers the costs of the Country Programme activities that occur when the Partner Organization disseminates infomation about Abilis funding, facilitates groups to apply funding, monitors on-going projects and provides training and advice t
o applicant and implemneting oragnizations. Activities are based on annual planning and are reported twice a year.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Increasing access of local people to public resources and govt. services/entitlements in regards to education, food and health in Nepal
rows.description:
Referring to the recently formed Federal Structural Governmental in Nepal, the project entitled Increasing access of local people especially disadvantaged groups to public resources and govt. services/entitlements in regards to education, food and health i
n Nepal” intends to make local people including women and indigenous people aware of the economic, social and cultural (ESC) rights alongside capacitating the newly elected local bodies on the issues. This helps in sensitizing the authorities to be more 
responsible towards the issues on public service delivery mechanism and existing issues need to address in changing structure and context, concerns with the local people. The project focuses on monitoring the local and provincial government's action plan a
nd guidelines, services delivery mechanism, if they are in line with the human rights based approach or not and also regarding their proper and effective implementation. The local governments have been granted sweeping powers both in terms of budget and th
e decision making powers. However, there is also a risk of such powers being abused as we have seen irregularities involving the all-party mechanisms in the past.  With the project’s initiative of capacitating both the local people and elected members, t
he project aims to minimize such risk and make the local government strengthen inter-relations among the federation, province and local level by distributing the resources and means judiciously. The newly elected representatives especially the judicial com
mittees will be capacitated to demonstrate efficient leadership by engaging citizens and local people in the development activities so as to develop a sense of ownership in local decision making and service delivery. This way, the project initiatives expec
t to enhance the relationship between right holder and duty bearers at the local and provincial level aiming to protect the fundamental human rights of the people.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Advancing the Rights of Forced Migrants in Nepal
rows.description:
In the changed political context of Nepal, there has been significant scope for the drafting, amending number of laws and policies of  various issues of human rights concern. The only issue that lacks the normative framework is of issues relating to forced
 migrants who categorically are refugees and asylum seekers. The project  is dedicated for the population who are in vulnerable conditions resulting from the absence of such laws and policies. The project aims at meeting the expected outcomes of legal prot
ection through four mutually reinforcing  approaches: i) empowering the refugee community by creating a pool of paralegals through a Paralegal Short Course ii) Instantaneous address of legal challenges faced by beneficiaries  through a law clinic and mobil
e legal clinics iii) Public advocacy through outreach, dialogues/discussions, information campaigns, and workshops and iv) Closed-door meetings with host community organizations including law enforcement and administrative officials, members of judiciary a
nd civil society, Community Based Organizations (CBOs)  and other relevant stakeholders not limiting up the official channels. These four-fold approaches will help the forced migrants to access justice, sustainably protect rights of their fellow communitie
s through paralegal short courses and well network with public officials to foster protection sphere. www.inhuredinternational.org

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Promotion of Access to Justice of Migrant Workers
rows.description:
After the promulgation of the constitution and restructuring of the state, a need has been realized for Nepal to come up with a plan of action to address the issues of migration shifting it from centralized institutional arrangments towards more progessive
 and participatory local governance dimensions. The overall goal envisioned by this project is protection and promotion of access to justice of migrant workers enforcing the policy level changes through engagement of wide ranges of experts and stakeholders
' orientation, advocacy and case law decision, changing the behaviours or practise of duty bearers and/or empower rights holders and protecting the  rights of the migrant workers and facilitate their case proceedings in local level governance.After the suc
cessful imlementation of the project, the migrant workers including their family members will have knowledge to access or access to justice in the changed context of State governance. Beside that, various stakeholders will have knowlege on the decentraliza
tion mandates of the Foreign Employment which will change in the skills, awareness ability and behaviour or practise of both duty bearers and right holders and other responsibles in the entire spectrum of migration process through the local governance. www
.peopleforum.org.np

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Building up capacity through mobilising peace cadres to support single women with their equal acces to social justice
rows.description:
The project supports WHR's work towards the SDG of ‘’achieving gender equality and empowering all women and girls, as well as promoting just, peaceful and inclusive societies.” Gender equality is not only a fundamental human right, but a necessary fo
undation for a peaceful, prosperous and sustainable society. Providing women with equal access to education, health care, decent work, and representation in political and economic decision-making processes will fuel sustainable economies and benefit societ
ies and humanity at large. By offering awareness and practical knowledge about the existing law and legal aid provisions to single women, as well as enhancing their ability to communicate with service providers to claim their rights, the project will lead 
towards greater economic, political and social empowerment for single women. Furthermore, the existing women’s network will be strengthened by raising the women’s capacity through educating, promoting and mobilising peace cadres” to act as focal pers
ons for single women to seek information and advice, as well support them to unite and organise themselves to play an active role within peacebuilding and social justice and to become change agents and role models in their communities, by confidently colla
borating with local stakeholders and the government to access their rights. http://whr.org.np/

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Promoting Human Right of GBV Survivors through Capacity Building Project
rows.description:
Justice delivered by the court is beyond the reach of ordinary people. It is expensive and cumbersome process which leads most justice seekers to drop the case. Hence, it is inaccessible to the poor and marginalized people, specially the victims of violenc
e. The project focuses on capacity building on human rights, legal rights and other related policy and direct support to individuals suffering from sexual violence. Similarly, it strengthens local judicial committees under the municipality and rural munici
palities to respond to GBV cases properly and effectively. Newly elected local-level representatives will be oriented on their roles with regards to addressing human rights issues in their respective jurisdictions (deputy mayors, vice chair-persons and mem
bers of municipalities and rural municipalities) exclusively on the human rights based approach (HRBA) to development, in particular the rights and responsibilities of local judicial communities, and dispute resolution and mediation process to ensure justi
ce to victims. This increases women’s access to justice. Mediation and Reconciliation Committees will also be constituted under the Judicial Committee. http://cvict.org.np/

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Women empowerment through sustainable access to finance and business skills
rows.description:
1. Project: 11865 Women Employment through Sustainable Access to Finance and Skills #2. Project area and Country: Sindhuli district, Nepal. #3. Project justification: #The higher concentration of minority and indigenous groups in the Sindhuli district has 
significantly contributed to the overall poverty levels of the local populations. High gender based disparity and discrimination have negative impacts on income opportunities and womens control of production assets in the targeted area. Also, womens access
 to information, finance and skill training opportunities are low in. Womens participation in local level decision-making is still rare in Sindhuli and one of the major obstacles for women to realize their social economic and political rights. Prevalent ge
nder based violence is a human rights violation and a strong barrier for women empowerment.#4. The main objective and the expected results:#The project aims to contribute to social and economic empowerment of women through the establishment and strengtheni
ng of women-led local cooperatives, capacity building and advocacy towards local government on womens rights. Women, particularly from the socially marginalized and economically poor groups, will increase their access to business/income opportunities throu
gh enhanced technical and entrepreneurship skills and better market linkages. The project will enhance rural womens access to finance through the establishment and strengthening of women owned and led cooperatives. Womens participation in decision making a
nd networking will be strengthened. This will contribute to womens enhanced access to public resources and services. The rights holders comprise of economically poor and socially marginalized groups, including indigenous ethnic and tribal groups such as Da
nuwar, Majhi and Magar.#5. Right-holders/ beneficiaries: 2900 persons (including 2400 women, 100 school drop-out girls and 400 other community people)#Duty bearers: local authorities and leaders, local institutions, community based organizations (CBOs) #6.
 Implementing partner: Sahakarya Nepal and Federation of Women Entrepreneurs Nepal (FWEAN)

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Advocating for consolidating of equality and non-discrimination for sustainable peace action
rows.description:
1. Project: 11882 Advocating for consolidation of Equality and Non-discrimination for Sustainable  Peace (ENDS) Action in Nepal #2. Project area and Country: Dhanusha and Mahottari districts, Nepal. #3. Project justification: #Caste based discrimination th
at affects in particular the Dalits, is the worst form of discrimination practiced in Nepal. The Dalits have limited access to education and information and their social participation is limited. The status of women and girls is low. Yet, they lack the cap
acity and skills to address these challenges. Their social mobility is limited and they have limited access to alternative livelihood opportunities and income. #The implementation of rights based on the provisions in the 2015 constitution and legislation i
s weak. Communities and their leaders lack knowledge and information about how to exercise their rights and fulfil their duties. In the coming years, policy advocacy will play a crucial role in influencing local laws and policymaking processes on behalf of
 the marginalized groups, as well as to establish platforms to promote dialogue between the people and the service providers. #4. The main objective and expected results:#The main objective of the project is to increase the participation of Dalits and the 
poorest rural communities in social and economic activities, local structures and decision-making that affect their lives. This will be accomplished through enhanced access to social and economic capital and opportunities and increased knowledge and eviden
ce based advocacy for the inclusion and equality of the target groups. The main activities include support to the implementation of small-scale community development schemes, entrepreneurship, business and skill trainings, support to local IGAs/MEs, awaren
ess raising on new governance structures and peoples roles and rights based on the provisions in the national legislation, networking and capacity building for local leaders, and evidence based advocacy toward the local government. #5. Right-holders/ benef
iciaries: 1932 targeted Dalits in Dhanusha and Mahottari districts #Duty bearers: District Administration Office, Nepal Police, justice providers and government offices #6. Implementing partner:  Nepal National Dalit Social Welfare Organization (NNDSWO)

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Economic and social empowerment
rows.description:
1. Project: 11887 Social and Economic Empowerment of Marginalized Groups including Freed Haliyas and Kamaiyas#2. Project area and Country: Kailali, Kanchanpur, Dadeldhura and Baitadi districts of Far West of Nepal #3. Project justification: #Far-western de
velopment region one of poorest area of Nepal. The area is home for ex-bonded labour such as Haliya, Kamaiya and Kamlaries who have still limited access to land, livelihood opportunities and basic social service facilities. Dalits (so called untouchable gr
oups) are another most marginalized groups living in the area. As they are occupational caste, they have very limited access to production resources such as land and capital to diversify livelihood opportunities. Single women are another category of vulner
able groups in Nepal. Due to deep-rooted patriarchal social architecture and prevalent social norms, single women have to face several attitudinal barriers limiting them to access services and opportunity to earn their livelihood.  #4. The main objective a
nd the expected results:#The main objective of the project is to strengthen social and economic status of most marginalized groups in Far Western region through promoting livelihood with access to finance and business literacy. The project aims to diversif
y income options for target groups through skill development, entrepreneurship training, enhanced production, value addition and marketing of farm and off farm-based products. Target groups, particularly vulnerable women, will enhance their access to finan
ce through affiliation in cooperative and capacity building in cooperative management and leadership. Strong networks of marginalized groups will be established at target villages and rural municipality level to advocate livelihood and rehabilitation issue
s towards local government and push forward their issues for higher-level advocacy as follow up against government commitment to these groups.   #5. Right-holders/ beneficiaries: 1200 targeted Freed Haliayas, Kamaiyas and Kamlaries, dalits and single women
 #Duty bearers: Cooperative Division, District Agriculture Office, District Cottage and Industry Office, Local bodies   #6. Implementing partner:  Freed Kamaiya Women Development Forum (FKWDF) and Integrated Development Society (IDeS)

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Nepal Country Programme
rows.description:
Nepals biodiversity is under constant threat from habitat degradation and fragmentation, deforestation and forest degradation, poaching and illegal wildlife trade and unsustainable infrastructure development. Vulnerability to natural disasters and climate 
change is remarkable. Threatened species such as tigers and snow leopards are threatened by poaching and illegal wildlife trade. Rural people are dependent on the natural resources for their daily needs to provide food, fodder, energy and water. Moreover, 
climate change has exacerbated the threats to biodiversity and peoples livelihoods. Theres also now window of opportunity to facilitate conservation mainstreaming in new federal structure.##Geographically programme focuses to the far-western parts of Nepal
 which belong under the Terai Arc Landscape. Programme aims to improve the forest corridor cover and connectivity as well as support buffer zone conservation and management through community-based conservation and cooperation with district forest officers.
 Improvement of the livelihoods and wellbeing of the local communities is gained through developing income generating activities such as forest-based enterprises and by increasing access to low carbon energy. In addition, we increase conservation awareness
 and empower local youth to act for the environment. We also support local communities to adapt in climate change by developing ecosystem based adaptation solutions. Programme tackles illegal wildlife trade and poaching by supporting community-based anti-p
oaching groups. ##Expected achievements by 2021# Around 3,000 ha of degraded forest is under improved management and restoration# Community forest groups and their members are capacitated and empowered to manage forests# Sustainable forest management plans
 are prepared and implemented for degraded forest area# At least 3 green community based enterprises are established and strengthened# 5,000 plus youth and children are made aware of conservation issues and challenges##Beneficiaries: the local community fo
rest user groups, women and youth groups, ethnic minority groups, forest workers and disadvantaged groups, local governments.##Implementor: WWF Nepal (www.wwfnepal.org)

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Women leadership in food sovereignty and environmental democracy in Nepal
rows.description:
The project aims at improved womens leadership in diversifying food production, harvesting and marketing systems through recognizing their roles and traditional knowledge of sustainable and resilient farming system. Additionally, the project aims at streng
thened womens voice and influence in local governments ensuring incorporation of gender inclusive agriculture and food sovereignty in development planning and implementation In Nepal.  Women groups within forest user groups and elected representatives in R
amechhap and Rautahat Districts are the direct stakeholders of the project, while policy makers in federal and state level, development practitioners, civil society organizations and men farmers benefit indirectly from it.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Encouraging Sustainable Livelihood of Marginalised Farmers in Rural Nepal
rows.description:
Nepal has been supplying 1500 unskilled youth per day for foreign labour work. This very popular youth (majority male) out-migration has resulted feminization of agriculture in the villages of Nepal. Also, a huge amount of farmland has been abandoned, and 
the remaining women farmers have moved away from multi crops / multi plants tradition to single crops / single plant practices in the name of commercialization of farming. That is how rural Nepal has been being converted from biodiversity friendly places t
o a completely different farmland. In this project aim is to advance following issues: #- Advance discussion on the importance of maintaining biodiversity #- Facilitate newly emerged women farmers to understand sustainable livelihood and benefits in mainta
ining biodiversity while doing farming.#- Let rural youths to have comparative perspectives of out-migration for similar unskilled jobs vs. continue doing farming in the village #- Facilitate understanding of newly elected leadership about the above mentio
ned themes.    #- Engage with other CBOs in the line of open space to popularise alternative ideas, plans and policies. #Following activities are planned:# Avail platform for rural women farmers to understand benefits of maintaining biodiversity for the hu
mankind, and how it helps increasing their incomes for their own livelihood. # Engage with youths (both returnees and aspirants for out-migration without any skills) so that the aspirants know the true picture of out-migration by the returnees, that eventu
ally encourages them not to go out for similar unskilled jobs but retain in the village. # Give publicity of above mentioned activities to encourage other women and youths from project uncovered areas not only by publishing newsletters, books, journal arti
cles, op-ed, etc., but also by organising regional and national consortiums and networks under the banner of Nepal Social Forum, Asian Social Forum, Forum for Sustainable Development, etc.    #Though the direct beneficiaries of the project will be rural wo
men, youths and elected representatives of a few villages, people of Nepal will be indirectly benefited from the project.#The project is implemented by SADED-Nepal in the rural areas of Kavre, Dolakha and Nuwakot Districts. A tentative plan is to cover 19 
village communities in the project. National dialogues and Social Forum related activities will be in Kathmandu and Lalitpur districts of Nepal.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Organizing Nepalese Construction Sector Migrant Workers
rows.description: The project focusing on migration leans on BWIs three strategic pillars: build (=organizing), defend (=negotiations) and promote (=influence).

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Formalizing Informal Work in the Health Sector
rows.description:
The objective of the project is to improve the rights, working conditions and job security of the female volunteer workers in the health care sector in Nepal. The trade unions mobilizing the volunteer health workers have created a joint trade union platfor
m, in order to jointly reinforce the mass power and strengthen the identity as real workers who deserve the benefits and remuneration for the tasks they perform. The weak situation of the volunteers will be raised into the national debate and people´s con
sciousness. The long-term aim of the project is to ensure that volunteer health workers are recognized as permanent public employees. In Nepal, there are 50 000 volunteer health workers, who do life-saving priceless work in communities under the Female Com
munity Health Volunteer (FCHV) -program. Their working terms are highly informal, despite the fact, that their work is to produce regular and vital public health services. The group of FCHVs represent the single largest segment of informal public services 
workers in Nepal. These women do not get any salary, only some of their work expenses are reimbursed, and they are economically in a very vulnerable position. The FCHVs do regular and vital work that saves lives of mothers and infants in rural areas, witho
ut any benefits of a normal employee status. As a result of the project, the unions create a joint charter of demands. Based on the charter and a baseline study conducted in 2017, an advocacy campaign to formalize the employment of the FCHVs is launched. A
 large part of the health volunteers work in rural areas and for that reason the information sharing and union communication will be extended geographically to cover also rural areas. Trade union leaders and activists are trained in strategic planning, cam
paigning and labor legislation. The project benefits the female community health workers of Nepal. The project is implemented by Public Services International (PSI) together with its member unions HEPON, NEVA and ANWHVWU.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: SUS: Uplift Community Development Program
rows.description:
Poverty is the core problem of the project areas and beneficiaries, resulting problems that include food insecurity and widespread malnutrition, illiteracy and lack of access to employment opportunities and inaccessibility to basic facilities such as water
 and sanitation. Most Dalits are deprived of their basic human rights such as citizenship and land-rights.  Similarly most children are deprived even of primary education, and there are cases of high early drop-outs. Caste and gender based discriminations 
against women and socially excluded people still prevail thus depriving them of public service delivery such as income generating, health, education, and access to social security benefits. The project, that covers three districts, Morang, Sarlahi and Chit
wan, aims to tackle these problems with the objective that the poor and excluded people are socially, economically and legally empowered.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Sanjal: Grass-root level work for peace
rows.description:
The project will strengthen local community networks in order to address causes of social, economic, and cultural injustices. The community members of different faiths, cultures, and ethnicities will live together in a peaceful and just society. The projec
t will work with and strengthen the local community institutions and systems to sustain project interventions. Youth, women and community leaders networks are formed to create an environment of collaboration for community peace building and conflict transf
ormation. In order to realize the importance of education among Muslim, Madeshi and children with disabilities, the project will support Madrasa by strengthening its management system.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: UMN Integrated Education Development Project
rows.description:
This project aims to bring fullness of life through sustainable and practical education and is implemented in Rukum. It is a poor district with very low in terms of education and HDI indicators. Untouchability, discriminations, inequity, linguistic barrier
s, access and gender are some of the issues that is highly prevalent in these districts. Rukum district was largely impacted by 10 years long conflict in the past as Rukum, Rolpa were center points for insurgency. Many people were displaced, children were 
orphaned and schools were dismantled. Almost a generation of children were deprived of quality education.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: CMC-N School Mental Health Program/Children
rows.description:
This project contributes in development of psychosocial focal teachers (one male and one female) and establishment of students psychosocial support unit in each project schools. The practice of child friendly classroom management, positive disciplinary app
roach, parenting education and parental counseling starts and child protection policy is in place in program schools. It ultimately promotes psychosocial wellbeing of school going children and adolescents. As a result, students school attendance and learni
ng achievement increases, reduces drop-out especially of the girl children, minimize the trends of early marriage, increases the school retention rate of children and finally contributes in quality learning. At the end, this project will have impact in qua
lity education and support in policy advocacy at local and central level to integrate school mental health component in school education system.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Koshish: Strengthening Stakeholder Capacity Psychosocial Disability Rights in Legal Framework
rows.description:
Persons with psychosocial disability face daily barriers in society, exacerbated by lack of psychosocial disability friendly laws in Nepal. KOSHISH carried out a study to outline the gaps in the Nepali legal framework within the context of the constitution
 of Nepal and UNCRPD. On the basis of the conclusions and recommendations of said study, KOSHISH has designed a comprehensive four year project which aims to support establishment of psychosocial disability friendly legal framework in Nepal which protects 
and promotes their rights. Needs based activities are outlined in this proposal for Members of Parliament, Bureaucrats, Constitutional Body members and Judges to take measures to include psychosocial disability in their agenda.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Sahas:  Mountain Community development programme
rows.description:
The project aims to improve the livelihood and food security situation of marginalized, resource poor, dalits, women and people with disabilities living in remote area of four districts. The  project builds the capacity of the community through institution
al/grassroots organisation and socio-political development, agriculture and livelihood improvement, and community infrastructure development.  Gender inclusion, people with disability, environment, climate change and disaster risk reduction (DRR) component
s are included as cross-cutting theme. The project is implemented in current working area of Gorkha, Dhading, Tanahu and Mugu in 2018 and then from 2019 the project will be implemented in new areas of the Gorkha, Tanahu and Dhading.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Coordination of Development Cooperation
rows.description:
The regional Development Manager contributes to ensure that the project planning, monitoring, evaluation and reporting complies with requirements. The regional Development Manager supports the local partners in project management and has an advisory functi
on in developing the quality of the projects. The regional Development Manager provides regularly information on project processes, local working context, and feedback from the local partners and the support they need, to the Development Cooperation Unit i
n Helsinki.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Towards Arsenic Safe Groundwater in Nepal
rows.description:
The presence of arsenic in groundwater is a common problem affecting the quality of drinking water on the plateaus of Nepal. Using water containing arsenic as drinking water in cooking or irrigation may cause a plethora of health issues even cancer. In Nep
al the work to reduce the levels of arsenic is fragmentary and lacking in cooperation and coordination between local officials. In addition knowledge about the health hazards of arsenic among the consumers of contaminated water is scarce.The Geological Sur
vey of Finland (GTK) and the Finnish Environment Institute?s (SYKE) shared project will strengthen coordination the sharing of information and cooperation between agents in different fields who have for now worked in silos. The Department of Water Supply a
nd Sewerage Management (DWSSM) whose responsibility is water quality will implement the Project as a partner organization.The immediate goal of the Project is to improve the partner organization?s capacity to guide and support stakeholders in the field of 
water management to prevent and reduce the prevalence and use of water contaminated by arsenic.The goal for the first output area is to strengthen the knowledge-based instruction of the DWSSM on diminishing of the presence of arsenic. This is attainable by
 enhancing the flow of information between different stakeholders developing effective processes for the handling of information as well as increasing the understanding of how geological conditions affect the quality of the water in Nepal. The second outpu
t area focuses on the DWSSM?s ability to use and adapt geological and hydrogeological research regarding the arsenic problem in ground water as a base for decision making. The third output area is to improve their tracking of arsenic levels and the practic
es of analysis of the data. Tighter cooperation and interaction between the DWSSM and stakeholders is also one of the output areas of the project. During the project a public communications plan on the arsenic issue will be developed for the department.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Finnpartnership programme
rows.description: Long term partnership with a local company / organisation (e.g. joint venture; subcontractor, import or other contract agreement )

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Strengthening and Expanding the Community Model of Mental Health Care in Nepal
rows.description:
Prevalence of mental health disorders is high in rural parts of Nepal and access to mental health services can be very limited due to challenges in awareness recognition and treatment options. The project aims to promote mental health and increase access t
o mental health services in public health network within the districts of Lamjung and Dhading. Specific aims are to 1) promote mental health awareness and reduce stigma related to problems with mental health 2) increase accessibility and effectiveness of m
ental health treatment in government health facilities and 3) empower community and people with mental disorders to advocate their rights and better access to treatment. The focus is on strengthening primary level outpatient treatment in line with WHO's re
commendations and Nepal's National Mental Health Policy of 2017. The project implements a similar model that was previously adapted to Dang district. The model is based on strengthening already existing public health structures from health posts to special
ized psychiatric hospitals by training health workers already publicly employed to recognize refer and treat mental health issues as well as raising citizens? awareness through workshops and advocacy. Local and district level mental health groups are estab
lished to promote the rights of mentally ill people and enable citizens participation in decision making. Public health network is engaged in project activities and meetings to ensure continuity of mental health services after end of the project. Lastly me
ntal health workshops are held for migrant workers in Kathmandu in collaboration with SASK. A qualitative study will be conducted to understand factors impacting migrant workers? mental health.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Unlocking doors to basic health and education for children with Sensory disabilities in Bara and Parsa districts of Nepal
rows.description:
In Nepal children are not screened for visual or hearing impairments and knowledge of eye- and ear health is generally poor. Having a disability is still greatly stigmatised. Although the majority of children in Nepal have access to education children with
 disabilties (CwD) are mostly excluded from it which is a major risk for marginalisation. The project is a follow-up of a 2-year pilot in Bara district (2017-18) which will be extended in Bara and Parsa. The aim is to increase the enrollment of 3 to 14-yea
r-old children with vision or hearing impairment in public schools. Districts have high rates of poverty and school attendance is very low. Also the human right's of CwD's are largely violated. The majority of pupils are boys therefore the project promotes
 girls' right to education. The project will educate female community health volunteers (FCHVs) to identify children with vision or hearing issues. The children identified through screening are examined at Eye + Ear Camps to diagnose the impairments found.
 It is estimated that the most of these impairments can be easily cured with simple treatments. Children with a severe visual or hearing disability will undergo a 4-6 month rehabilitation to enable life outside home and attending school. Awareness raising 
campaigns regarding eye- and ear health as well as rights of persons with disabilities (PwD) will be organised among local citizens during the camps. Advocacy on inclusive education and human rights of CwD will be promoted. Eye + ear care and rehabilitatio
n centers will be established in Bara. The local project partner is B. P. Eye Foundation (BPEF) that has 3 decades of experience in rehabilitation of CwDs and training of ophtalmologists and optometrists. For improved access to treatment and rehabilitation
 and to increase awareness on human rights gender equality and inclusive education the project will strengthen the collaboration with other NGO's: IMPACT Nepal Nepal Association for the Welfare of the Blind Nepal Apanga Sewa sangh Bara and Loo Niva. A refe
rral system will be developed with Patan CBR to reach a larger scale of PwD's. In Finland we will collaborate with Interpedia ry.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Promoting Right to Education and Protection of the Most Vulnerable Children (PREP)
rows.description:
With this project Interpedia Loo Niva and Patan CBR wish to see that in the long run all children regardless of their disability sex caste or religion enjoy their childhood in a safe supportive and inclusive environment in Mahankal and Konyosome Rural Muni
cipalities of Lalitpur District. To achieve that long term impact the project aims in four years (2021-2024) to ensure that:1) Project municipalities and provincial government adopt and implement disability inclusive education and child protection policies
 (Outcome 1). 2) All 40 public schools (primary and secondary) of the project area are safe supportive accessible and inclusive for all including girls Dalits and Children with Disabilities (Outcome 2)3) Local communities are safe supportive and inclusive 
to all children specifically girls Dalit and CwDs (Outcome 3)4) Project staff delivers high quality rights-based work performance and partners are able to lead child rights movements and networks (Outcome 4)The project takes a human rights progressive appr
oach to development creating a systems-level dialogue between rights holders and duty bearers raising the capacity of local duty bearers and rights holders to ensure the sustainability of results. The project will take place in ten wards (smallest administ
rative unit of Nepal's governance structure) covering 40 schools and all villages. The final beneficiaries are children with disabilities and their families Dalit children and girls.Work is done with elected members of wards rural municipalities and provin
cial government officials in rural municipality and provincial level education and social affairs offices school teachers principals hired staff students parents community leaders parents of children with disabilities and out-of-school children with disabi
lities Dalits and girls. Collaboration with community-based organisations and other NGOs active in the area is done to ensure synergy and avoid duplication.Communities as an entity will be reached with innovative Pidhi Kakshya- method where awareness raisi
ng is done by community members in the community itself thus also reaching the hard-to-reach audience.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Strengthening livelihoods civil society and community-based natural resource management of indigenous communities
rows.description:
Chepang and Tamang communities living in the hills of central Nepal are among the most marginalized and resource poor groups in Nepal.Their tiny land holdings provide no more than 3 to 6 months of food security and deforestation has contributed to environm
ental degradation. Due to lack of knowledge of efficient and sustainable agricultural practices and land-tenure rights the communities are practicing unsustainable agriculture and animal husbandry which aggravate soil erosion that causes regular landslides
 that destroy agricultural fields and threaten the lives of people and animals. To improve access to remote villages local government has started infrastructure development projects without proper social and environmental impact assessments.The objective o
f this project is to uplift the livelihoods and food security of the Chepang and Tamang communities through agro-forestry development and developed market access strenghten community-based natural resource management sustainable land use and climate change
 adaptation and empower women and persons with disabilities.This is achieved by e.g. developing sound biodiversity management and sustainable livelihood activities in cooperation with community-based organizations and local government. Capacity building of
 forest user groups will be done to strenghten their operational capacity and local government will be supported in preparation and implementation of the local climate change adaptation plan of action.The continuation project implemented by NAFAN and suppo
rted by Swallows supports Finland's development objectives by increasing food security and promoting sustainable use of natural resources local democracy and participation of women in decision making. The project will strengthen local partner organization'
s capacity and role in community capacity building and establishing linkages between communities and local government. The project is expected to benefit directly around 3000 right-holders and 150 duty-bearers. In addition approx. 3500 people are expected 
to benefit indirectly.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Mother Tongue Based Basic Education Program into Lhomi language
rows.description:
This project seeks to improve Lhomi children?s right to quality learning increase efficiency of learning and develop their cognitive skills to attain desired learning outcomes. Some 700 Lhomi children and their parents will directly benefit within the proj
ect period.About 6000 Lhomi people who speak Lhomi as their first language live in the Himalayas in Nepal. There is total of 14 public schools in which there are in total about 150 pupils per grade level. When starting school these children have little or 
no knowledge of the Nepali language which is used as the language of tuition. Children in Nepal have the right to free education in their own language. For Lhomi children this right is not currently materialized. Partially because of the language barrier c
hildren are not achieving the desired learning outcomes. This also contributes to high dropouts.The previous phase of this project focused on improving the quality of education in the pre-primary level. A large proportion of the children involved have atta
ined basic literacy. In order to achieve minimum proficiency levels in reading and math there is a need for further development of Lhomi education.This project is seeking to improve the rest of the primary school from grade 2 to 5. The main objective of th
is intervention is to improve learning outcomes in public schools. Quality mother tongue-based learning materials will be produced and all teachers will be trained. It is important that the local key stakeholders become actively engaged. Therefore parents 
teachers school management committees and education authorities will be trained to become aware of their responsibilities and motivated to improve children?s learning. In each school there will be awareness raising meetings for parents. In these community 
events parents - mainly mothers - and teachers will discuss education and other children's wellbeing issues.Wycliffe Raamatunkääntäjät (WRK) partners with Nepal Lhomi Society (NELHOS) who is responsible for implementation. No Finnish personnel are invo
lved in the operational implementation of the project. WRK and NELHOS have 10 years of shared experience on similar projects.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: WOMEN FOR ENTREPRENEURSHIP AND RESILIENCE ? transforming fish-farming and forest value-chains in Nepal
rows.description:
The FFD project ?WOMEN FOR ENTREPRENEURSHIP AND RESILIENCE ? transforming fish-farming and forest value-chains in Nepal? aims to enhance women?s income generation entrepreneurship and livelihood resilience. In Chitwan and Dang districts forestry and fish-f
arming are male dominated sectors where decision-power and access to direct benefits are held by men. Traditional roles low education levels and lack of targeted services limit women?s opportunities in these sectors. Climate and non-climate hazards aggrava
te livelihood insecurity and especially women in the marginalized groups are in risk to fall beyond safety nets.The project enables women to seize a centric role in terms of decision-making and direct economic benefits in fish-farming and forest value-chai
ns. Farmers organizations hold a crucial role in breaking discriminating structures and transforming production towards resilient pathways. The project engages 18 organizations which commit to non-discrimination provide targeted services for women and boos
t the presentation of their 1200 women members? interest. New policy mechanisms are established linking women producers with local governments? decision making. Local organizations engage their members and communities (5450 men and women) to embrace rights
 and responsibilities and to seize emerging opportunities in climate-smart production value-addition and bio-products.Women improve resilience of fish-farming and non-timber forest products NTFP value-chains as the farmers organizations promote risk mitiga
tion and climate-smart practices. Women identify innovations new products and services forming income generation opportunities and entrepreneurship.FFD implements the project in Nepal in partnership with Federation of Community Forestry Users FECOFUN Women
?s Fish-Farming Cooperative Sundardeep and Forestry cooperative ASEC. FECOFUN is FFD?s main partner responsible for local coordination expertise and advocacy in Nepal. Sundardeep and ASEC implement the project activities together with their members and net
works. Finnish Fish Farming Association (FFFA) and FFD support the project partners with technical advisory services.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Improved food security sustainable livelihoods and empowerment of women
rows.description:
Bajhang is one of the least developed districts of Nepal where almost 50 % of people live below poverty line and suffer from food insecurity. Agricultural productivity is low due to lack of technical knowledge and unavailability of quality inputs and irrig
ation facilities. There are natural resources available but due to limited knowhow and poor market access people are not able to use them for livelihood improvement purpose. In the patriarchal communities women suffer from gender dicsrimination and violenc
e and are excluded from decision-making.The objective of this project is to uplift the livelihoods and food security of the socially and economically marginalized communities of Masta Rural Municipality through improved farming practices value addition and
 better access to markets empower women through capacity development and leadership trainings and build more resilient communities through sustainable animal husbandry and natural resource management and developing NTFP-based livelihood opportunities.The p
roject implemented by Sahara Nepal and supported by Swallows supports Finland's development objectives by increasing food security and promoting sustainable use of natural resources and enhancing the rights and status of women and girls. The project will s
trengthen local partner organization's capacity and role in community capacity building and establishing linkages between communities and local government. The project is expected to benefit directly around 300 poor households. At least half of the benefic
iaries will be women.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: ADHICAR - Enhancing Dignified Life of Freed Haliyas in Nepal
rows.description:
The action is designed to answer to some of the most severe needs and challenges of the freed Haliya communities women within Haliya and PwDs in the targeted communities in the context of the new federal structure and to offer means to their respective CSO
s to demand and advocate for the fulfilment of their rights. Through capacitating and empowering the selected CSOs to have suitable tools and frameworks for evidence-based advocacy the most marginalized people groups will have an opportunity to become agen
ts for change and participate contribute and benefit from the newly established federal structures and processes.The project will strengthen collective voices and participation of freed Haliya women within the freed Haliya community and PwDs as rights hold
ers to fulfil their rights and to access entitlements as provisioned in the laws policies and programs. The institutional capacity of CSOs DPOs freed Haliya groups and CBOs will be strengthened and as a result they will have the means to advocate in a stru
ctured manner for their rights.Through capacity building and building dialogue as well as cross learning visits political participation in the democratic practices will be increased. In the 2022 elections the political representation of Haliyas is expected
 to raise thus providing new pathways for promotion of rights within the democratic system. Moreover the target groups will be engaged in extensive ESC rights monitoring through ESCR mobile app. The local co-applicants will gain increased technical and man
agement capacity through the collaboration within the consortium and they can take this knowledge further to their partners during and after the completion of the action.The activities will be implemented together with all the applicants of the Action and 
in cooperation with the rights holders duty bearers and stakeholders of the Action.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: A Green and Strong West
rows.description:
The planned multi-donor co-operation project between Finland Germany and the EU is in line with the new Nepal Country Program 2021-2024 Impact area 1 that focuses on communities? improved climate resilience and health through safe and sustainable water and
 sanitation services and livelihood development based on natural resources.The impact of the project is the growth of climate resilient  and the green economy in the Far West and Karnali provinces. The project has two medium-term objectives the first of wh
ich Increased profitable participation in value chains that are based on sustainable and climate-resilient agricultural ecosystems. The second outcome is Improved functionality of the target value. Finnish Luke is participating in the research component of
 the project. The total budget of the project is approximately 13.4 Meur. This is a grant equivalent to an intergovernmental organization.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: BETTER CHILDHOOD BETTER FUTURE IN NEPAL
rows.description:
Nepal has a variety of development challenges related to the nonfulfillment of human rights that need to be addressed. This Country Programme aims to catalyse and equip the local churches and other civil societies to act as strong societal advocates to ful
fil the rights of vulnerable children and youth. In local partnership with AGN churches, Rescue Nepal, Relative Nepal, Transformation Nepal and New Life Service Association, the Country Programme improves children and other beneficiary rights, especially i
n regards to their rights to health, education, and adequate living standards. The health component of the Country Programme is addressing the health situation, hygiene and sanitation, and unsafe drinking water that have resulted in the poor health situati
on in village development areas of Basauti, Pabera, Gadariya, Pahalmanpur and Udasipur in the Kailali district. The Education component of the Country Programme is addressing the improvement on the quality of basic education, school management skills, know
ledge and skills of parents in schools, and village development areas of Ishworpur Muncipality in Sarlahi district, in Ramgorpalpur and Hattisarwa in the Mahottari district, Janakpur Muncipality and in Bateshwar, Bagchaura, Mithileshwar Nikas, Bhutahi Pate
rwa, Lagmagadhaghuthi and Tulsiyahi Nikash in the Dhanusha district. The adequate standard of living component of the Country Programme addresses the strengthening of institutional facilities and providing awareness and skills to improve livelihood of the 
families in Pokali, Ragani, Khijichandeshwori, Khijifalate and Khijikati village development areas of the Okhaldhunga district. The capacity building and advocacy components of the Country Programme are implemented with and within the AGN Church denominati
on. On the grass root level, the AGN church partner is a motivated social and development actor throughout Nepal. The Country Programme is expected to directly benefit 57 000 people and 305 000 people indirectly already during the Programme period. Moreove
r, the Programme will have many multiplier effects for the development of Nepal.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Sustainable WASH for all
rows.description:
Sustainable WASH for all in Nepal (SUSWA) strengthens the federalized governance system to guarantee the access to basic safe and sustainable drinking water sanitation services and improved hygiene practices at households schools public institutions and al
l people in general including marginalized groups. The project is a bilateral project between the governments of Finland and Nepal. The Finnish funding is 9 million euros and it will run between 2019-2023.SUSWA has four Result Areas: 1)  WASH governance at
 local level 2) Water Supply 3) Sanitation and hygiene and 4) Sector support and enabling environment.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: UNICEF WASH and Education Programmes
rows.description:
The Finnish Country Strategy for Development Cooperation in Nepal reduces poverty and promotes sustainable and equal development on three impact areas: 1) water supply and sanitation 2) high-quality education for all and 3) improving the rights and status 
of women and vulnerable groups. This project focuses on supporting UNICEF?s Country Programme Action Plan 2018-2022 (CPAP) for the Water and Sanitation Sector in Nepal. The Action Plan strengthens all the impact areas of the Finnish Country Strategy and in
creases the synergies between them. Finland has supported UNICEF in Nepal in 2010-2018 with 12 MEUR. The project had significat impacts in sanitation coverage in Nepal. Finland supported UNICEF with 2 MEUR in the reconstruction of water and sanitation syst
ems after the earthquake. As a result over 99 500 people benefitted from reconstructed/improved water systems and over 300 schools were supported in upgrading of their WASH facilities. The total budget for the intervention is 8 million euros for the years 
2018-2022. The comments of the quality assurance board have been included in the project proposal.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Supporting Democracy through Digital Investigative Journalism in Nepal
rows.description:
This continuation project has its former partner organisation and a new content digital journalism. The immediate objective of this project is to impart digital investigative journalism skills to journalists. This 2017-2020 project is a) to create a pool o
f investigative journalists skilled in the use of multi-media and data journalism tools b) to spread that know-how to the regional media and strengthen their investigative journalism content. Nepal's media landscape is diversifying with the arrival of new 
media platforms including online news portals and social media providing an effective alternate medium. To stay effective and relevant in this changing landscape investigative reporters need to adapt to and adopt these skills. The former project 2013-2015 
taught basic skills in investigative journalism to 70 journalists in various regions of the country. The shifting eyeballs towards digital portals in the past years has shown Nepalis now have an alternative outlet of publication and syndication of investig
ative stories that would otherwise have been sensitive for the corporate media. However the use of digital interfaces that use multimedia and datajournalism tools is deficient. The evaluation report of the earlier project between CIJ and VIKES has stated n
ecessity for a training based on feedback from trainee reporterstrainers and editors working in various Nepali media. Accordingly the proposed project aims at training journalists to adopt digital tools for investigating and reporting on matters crucial to
 common interest. The project will also support CIJ to develop its own capacity to train and mentor  investigative journalists in the use of multimedia and datajournalism techniques.  Democratic ownership of development is a crucial element in human rights
 -based development.  For citizens to know their rights and be able to exercise them they need an independent and critical media that serves their right to information. This is effectively being done in developed countries through promotion of investigativ
e journalism. The project aims to do the same in Nepal through its activities.

rows.title: TO ENHANCE THE SOCIO-ECONOMIC CONDITION AND ENABLE CLIMATE-SMART LIVELIHOOD INTERVENTIONS OF VULNERABLE AND MARGINALISED GROUPS RESIDING IN FOUR COMMU
rows.description: The project will enable targeted groups to increase their economic security through diversified livelihood strategies including introducing better climate-smart agricultural practices. There is a strong focus on local ownership. The project will support the building of local expertise but will also further develop the capacity of Nepal Red Cross staff and volunteers who will implement the project and then provide longer-term support to maintain the project outcomes. The Irish Red Cross expects that the initiatives supported by the project will prepare individuals and communities to be more resilient to the risks they face, with a range of practices that will enable them to anticipate, adapt and respond to situations.

ILGA World expands its global, regional, and national research through the project, including our SOGIESC-Database and the launch of the ILGA World Monitor, an automatic source aggregator that collects, translates, sorts, and labels content generated all around the globe relevant to SOGIESC communities.

rows.title: Negotiated Partnership: Save the Children New Zealand (SCNZ) - Implementation
rows.description: Implementation of a multi-year, multi-country, child and youth-focused Negotiated Partnership with Save the Children NZ. The programme builds resilience to disasters and climate risk through safe schools, ensures that families can meet health, nutrition and educational needs and that children face less harm from all forms of violence.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Strengthening Human Rights of Dalit Women in Nepal
rows.description:
The projects aims to increase awareness among Dalit women on human rights, national and international obligations of the state and other pertinent issues through evidence-based advocacy, to empower existing and future Dalit women leaders for ensuring econo
mic rights of Dalit women with concrete economic empowerment initiatives and to create an enabling environment for increasing access of Dalit women to justice at the local level. The activities are expected to be instrumental in developing leadership of Da
lit women, changing the political landscape and contributing to the protection and promotion of social and economic rights of Dalit women. www.fedonepal.org

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Enhancing knowledge and leadership capacity of youths for appropriate recognition of bio-cultural rights in Federal Nepal
rows.description:
Hundreds of communities in different eco-regions (mountain, hill and low land tarai) of the country, are maintaining bio-cultural conservation practices in Nepal. Appropriate legal recognition of bio-cultural practices of these communities has become one o
f the crucial but critical policy issues in Nepal. Numbers of new legislations are under formulation with little inputs from forest communities, civil society organisations including the youths. Studies, knowledge and capacities of youths are vital for the
 sustainability of bio-cultural conservation practices and critical and constructive public policy debates in Nepal's ongoing policy making processes. However, presence as well as voices of the youths in bio-cultural conservation practices and ongoing publ
ic policy debates is completely absent. This project is developed to contribute in this policy gap. The expected result of the proposed project is better functioning and appropriate recognition of the bio-cultural conservation practices of forest communiti
es through enhanced knowledge and leadership capacity of youths for the effective and sustainable bio-cultural conservation practices and constructive public policy debates in federal Nepal. This project aims to develop knowledge and leadership capacity of
 youths by facilitating them in self-documentation, self-analysis and self-dissemination of the knowledge on bio-cultural conservation practices so as to promote their effective participation in conservation practices and networking with concerned CSOs for
 public policy debates. The youths studying in grade 9 to 12 in four piloting sites (representing three eco-regions of the country) and all the community members in the respective piloting sites will be direct beneficiaries of the proposed project. Similar
ly, all the members of ICCA Network Nepal (an informal civil society network formed by 29 local forest and conservation communities-"Indigenous and Community Conserved Areas-ICCA") including related CSOs will also be benefited from the collaboration and pa
rtnership for policy advocacy and lobby. Project is implemented by  Ribdung-Jaleswori Education Foundation (REF), www.ref.org.np.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Ecosystem and Community Forestry Management through Rural and Indigenous Women’s Leadership
rows.description:
Implementing organisation WATCH aims to promote a holistic approach to ecosystem management that takes into account family-level development, agricultural production concurrently with other benefits such as soil and water quality, biological conservation, 
and increased resilience of rural communities. The project aims to develop ecosystem management plans for community forest user groups. Community forest groups have become less aware of their rights and many groups are almost defunct and under the guidance
 of a few community representatives. The project is being implemented on a pilot basis among women's groups and community forest groups in Kathmandu and Rupandeh districts. The beneficiaries of the project are 10 community forest user groups (comprising ap
proximately 25000 family members as indirect beneficiaries) and 25 women groups (totaling approximately 1000 women). There are an estimated 2,000 direct beneficiaries, 70-80% of whom are women. The project aims at recognizing biocultural rights of indigeno
us people in resource management and mitigation and adaptation of disasters induced by climate change and unplanned development. The project aims at the following outputs: 20 group level ecosystem management plans and two regional level plans based on grou
p plans; 10 Community Forest Management Plans; Coordination and cooperation with local governments for plans implementation; and  Increased awareness of communities about biocultural rights, ecosystems and community forestry. Project is implemented by Wome
n Acting Together for Change (WATCH), http://www.watch.org.np.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Securing Bio-cultural Rights of IPLCs in the National ABS Policies and Community Forest Management Plans in Nepal
rows.description:
The securing of biocultural rights of indigenous and local communities (IPLC) in Nepal's  national policies and legislation and management plan of community forestry is one of the challenging issue for IPLCs in Nepal. Despite lots of community efforts, sti
ll there are lacking explicit legal provisions on bio-cultural rights of IPLCs in the national policies and local level forest management plans. The aim of this project is to secure these rights in legislation as well as in policies and plans at different 
levels of government. The project has the following dimensions: Local level training in the western high hill region and in the border region with India(western Terai Arc landscape); A national level workshop on biocultural rights in Kathmandu; Documentati
on and mapping of traditional knowledge, customary practices and bio-cultural practices as well as revision of forest management plan for securing bio-cultural rights will be conducted in two community forest groups (Myagdi and Bardiya districts); Based on
 the material collected in the project, a resource booklet on biocultural rights will be published in Nepali and a summary in English; The lessons learned from the project would be shared at the CBD COP15 conference in China in 2020. The main expected resu
lt of the project is to incorporate the policy and legal provisions on bio-cultural rights in the national policy and legislation and to establish functional practices for the securing bio-cultural rights in the local communities as well as national level.
 The IPLCs will be able to claim their bio-cultural rights based on their community protocols or forest management plans. The main beneficiaries of the project are indigenous communities and community forest groups covered by the project. Other key stakeho
lders in the project include other community-based organisations in project communities, other NGOs involved in workshops and training, and government officers at different levels. The aim is to have more than 140 people participating in trainings and work
shops. In addition, about 230 households would benefit from biocultural surveys and updated forest management plans. Project is implemented by Federation of Community Forestry Users Nepal (FECOFUN) -järjestö,  www.fecofun.org.np.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Resilience Programme
rows.description: Community resilience programme: supports rural and remote Nepalese villages in increasing disaster preparedness, health awaraness and access to water and sanitation

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Economic empowerment for social justice: support efforts of the women in Southern Lalitpur to scale up business initiatives and combat inequality
rows.description:
1. Project: 12057 Economic empowerment for social justice: Supporting efforts of the women in Southern Lalitpur to scale up their business initiatives and combat inequality.2. Project area and Country: Southern Lalitpur, Nepal                              
              3. Project justification: The social and development indicators of Lalitpur district as a whole, in general, are above the average status of Nepal. Nevertheless, the Southern part of the Lalitpur district is characterized by limited access, h
igh concentration of the socially marginalized groups (Janajatis, Dalits), the prevalence of the caste and gender-based discrimination and inequalities including domestic violence resulting into higher poverty and marginalization. Women are one of the most
 marginalized groups with very limited participation, network, and opportunity. Moreover, the control of women in production resources is extremely low.  4. The main objective and the expected results:The main objective is to increase influence and dignity
 of women in southern Lalitpur through consolidation of achievements on access to finance, business and market skills, and promoting gender justice. To achieve this goal, the project will enhance the access of women to affordable finance through consolidat
ion of capacity of twelve women’s cooperatives. Thematic trainings to key stakeholders will include account management, product and business development, gender based violence (GBV) reduction, inclusion of people living with disabilities, etc. The projec
t will carry out several advocacy activities to overcome GBV and promote meaningful participation of women in the communities by implementing family and CBOs dialogues targeting men of the localities. Additionally, there will be a close cooperation with th
e GBV sub-committees and Judicial Committee at the local government on monitoring of GBV cases, reporting child marriage and supporting women victims of violence to access justice. Likewise, advocacy will take place at local government level for encouragin
g local producers to promote pest-free production.5. Right-holders/beneficiaries: 4,500 women Duty bearers: Rural municipalities, local government’s agencies, CBOs 6. Implementing partner: Society of Local Volunteers’ Effort (SOLVE) and Federation of W
omen Entrepreneurs’ Association of Nepal (FWEAN)

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Empowering Forced Migrants Through Enabling Interventions
rows.description: The project seeks to improve the protective framework of and empower forced migrants in Nepal through a six-fold approach including legal aid, advoacy, publications and education. www.inhuredinternational.org

rows.title: F.a: Promotion of Rights of Migrant workers (Phase II)
rows.description: The project's main goals are the promotion of the rights of migrant workers by bringing policy changes at the local level through the engagement of stakeholders  and advocacy as well as orientating migrants workers on their rights. www.peopleforum.org.np
